title: Functions should not be declared at block scope
description: It is rarely useful to declare a function at block scope. Such a function will not get special access to any name in its enclosing scope, and therefore, it is equivalent but clearer to declare it instead in the enclosing namespace. Additionally, when a function is declared at block scope, the intent is often not to declare a function but instead to declare and initialize a variable.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Functions should not be declared at block scope. Move the declaration to namespace scope or consider if this should be a variable declaration instead.
